samples.summary: AI needs to be connected to the physical world, proud to be supporting ! William Fedus @LiamFedus Today, @ekindogus and I are excited to introduce @periodiclabs . cluster_samples.cluster_title: Speculation on brain upload via LLMs
core_facts: Andrej Karpathy argues that a lossy, approximate brain upload via LLM simulators is imminent and describes a hypothetical startup.
AI at Meta announced that the Muse Spark API will be released soon, noticing excitement among developers.

cluster_samples.cluster_title: Junyang Lin's reflections on the agent era
core_facts: Junyang Lin shares three thoughts on the agent era: critical thinking with agents, designing well-structured organizations, and newbies having advantages.
SWE-Bench Pro 64.3%, SWE-Bench Verified 87.6%, TerminalBench 69.4%. Coding performance improved meaningfully in Opus 4.7 vs 4.6.
Jan Leike tweets that most alignment research is not crisp and requires research taste, explaining why AAR focuses on scalable oversight.

cluster_samples.cluster_title: This life is fucking amazing. I'm so grateful to be alive, with all of you on this miracle of a plan...
core_facts: Lex Fridman writes a long personal tweet expressing gratitude, discussing world leader conversations, personal struggles, and plans to talk to more everyday people.

cluster_samples.cluster_title: Release of Mem0 OpenClaw Plugin v1.0.4
core_facts: Taranjeet announces Mem0 OpenClaw Plugin v1.0.4 with long-term memory features.
Dia browser advertises a feature to compare 15 open tabs and make a decision.
Mike Krieger of Anthropic Labs discusses building agent-native products, team structures, and the need to iterate quickly.
From browsing to doing (video).

cluster_samples.cluster_title: LiteLLM compromise and implications for agentic software security
core_facts: Discussion on how agentic frameworks pose new security risks, referencing compromised LiteLLM release.

core_facts: AI SDK has passed 10 million downloads per week.

cluster_samples.cluster_title: AI-for-code continues to grow while other verticals lag
core_facts: Adam D'Angelo observes that code keeps getting 100x bigger while other AI verticals grow slower, contrary to earlier predictions.

reasons: Use dry-run reports for manual review before write-real-db.

Collect more source_signals before trusting source_profile priority suggestions.
